description: Avatar asset set registry — cosmetic-only player profile portraits for the client UI and match opponent rail. 20 PNGs in 5 rarity tiers. Indexed by (num, name, rarity) tuple; Player 1 uses a designated default; AI players 2-8 each get a random distinct avatar assigned once at match start.

pathDiscrepancy.note: PNGs physically live at packages/client/assets/avatars/ (not src/assets/avatars/ as initially requested). This is the correct location — Vite v5 asset resolution will glob relative to the project root via import.meta.glob.

globStrategy.rationale: Client asset directory (assets/) is a sibling of src/. Mirrors the pattern already used by sprites.ts (which handles public/units/ via UNIT_ART_BASE = '/units') but for bundled assets instead of public-served files. Vite's import.meta.glob resolves relative to the importing file, so '../../../assets/avatars/*.png' from packages/client/src/ reaches packages/client/assets/avatars/. The glob returns a record<path,fn> of lazy-loaded modules; eager:true makes them import immediately. Parsed filenames feed the registry.
globStrategy.alternativeNotConsidered: Moving files to public/avatars/ was rejected because avatars are bundled cosmetic assets tied to profile selection (not arbitrary drop-in slots like unit/item art). Bundling keeps the set finite and versionable.

groups.name: Avatar display name (Whiskers, Biscuit, etc.)
groups.rarity: Cosmetic tier: common | uncommon | rare | epic | legendary

player1DefaultDesign.rationale: Player 1 (the human) always shows the designated default. Recommendation: make this a module-level export constant PLAYER_1_AVATAR_NUM = 1, so any future player profile system can override it. PNGs include rarity-ring artwork; no secondary ring should be stacked on rendering.

inMainMenu.nextAction: replace with resolveAvatarTexture(PLAYER_1_AVATAR_NUM) or fallback to glyph if avatar system not yet initialized

inOpponentRail.currentImpl: empty panelBg circle (8px radius) + text label
inOpponentRail.nextAction: replace the panelBg disc with clipped avatar image from the registry; fallback to the current glyph if avatar not yet assigned/loaded

exports: AvatarEntry: { num: 1–20, name: string, rarity: AvatarRarity, url: string } (type)
resolveAvatarTexture(num: number, lookup): T | null (pure resolver, mirrors sprites.ts pattern)
avatarPath(num: number, base?: string): string (url builder)
requestAvatarArt(num: number, base?: string): void (async loader + cache, mirrors requestUnitArt)
registryModuleSpec.pattern: Mirrors sprites.ts exactly: pure resolution (path→texture|null) split from runtime loading (async cache + listener broadcast). PNGs live in assets/avatars/; glob parses filename; exports the list + resolve functions. No game logic — cosmetic only.

mainMenu.ringRing: NONE — PNGs include rarity ring artwork. Do not stack a second ring.

opponentRail.content: AvatarEntry texture corresponding to assigned avatar number or fallback glyph
opponentRail.ringRing: NONE — PNGs already have rarity ring. Current ring styling (chipBorder / textCombat for current opponent) must NOT be stacked on top; instead, the PNG becomes the visual and the stroke is removed.
opponentRail.assignmentStrategy: At match start (when MatchState is created, before planning begins), assign each AI player 2–8 a random distinct avatar from the pool excluding Player 1's selection. Store assignment in a client-side match-scoped map: seatNum → avatarNum. On every opponent-rail render, look up the stored assignment.
opponentRail.note: AI players persist same avatar across all rounds of a single match; human player always shows PLAYER_1_AVATAR_NUM

coder: 1. Create avatars.ts module at packages/client/src/avatars.ts with the glob + parse + pure resolver (mirrors sprites.ts).
2. Export PLAYER_1_AVATAR_NUM = 1 as an editable constant at the top of avatars.ts.

3. In packages/client/src/scenes/match.ts renderOpponentRail(): store MatchState seed in a client-local Map seatNum → avatarNum (generated once at match init via a helper in avatars.ts or passed in). When rendering each tile, call requestAvatarArt(avatarNum) and resolveAvatarTexture(avatarNum, runtimeLookup); fallback to the existing glyph if resolved to null.

4. In packages/client/src/ui/app.ts (main-menu identity cluster, .mm-avatar-frame): replace the person glyph with requestAvatarArt(PLAYER_1_AVATAR_NUM) + resolveAvatarTexture(PLAYER_1_AVATAR_NUM, ...). Fallback to glyph if not yet loaded.
5. DO NOT add a second ring/stroke on top of the avatar disc. PNGs include rarity artwork; keep the frame minimal (transparent background inside the circle).
6. Test: verify PNGs load correctly, avatars persist across rounds in a match, Player 1 avatar matches the main-menu identity and the opponent-rail seat-1 tile.

completeness: ALL 20 AVATARS PRESENT ON DISK. No missing assets. Ready for registry implementation.
